compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Why

The `optionality` workflow (lean-runtime / clean-core-wheel / per-extra / union checks)
casts NO Gerrit `Verified` vote — only `gerrit-verify.yaml` does, and it installs
`.[dev]` (heavy stack present), so the no-extras clean-wheel RUNTIME check never runs
pre-merge. A module-scope heavy-import regression (pydantic_ai/httpx) recently reached
main this way. The static AST guard already runs in Verified via `make test`; the
runtime/packaging jobs do not. Cost to add: ~0 critical-path latency (32s parallel vs
~1235s macOS long pole), ~145 cheap Linux machine-seconds/run.

## What

Extract optionality's jobs into a `workflow_call` reusable workflow and invoke it from
both the push/PR lane and the Verified lane, so a clean-wheel/packaging failure blocks
the Verified vote. Incorporate the critical-review findings (mandatory items below).

## Scope

- New `.github/workflows/_optionality.yml` (`on: workflow_call`, inputs for the checkout
  mode), `.github/actions/smart-checkout` composite, thin `optionality.yml` caller,
  `gerrit-verify.yaml` gains an `optionality` job + it is added to `vote`'s `needs`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `optionality`'s jobs live in a reusable `workflow_call` workflow, invoked by both
      `optionality.yml` (push/PR, branch-head checkout) and `gerrit-verify.yaml`.
- [ ] The gerrit lane checks out the EXACT patchset via `checkout-gerrit-change-action`
      (never plain `actions/checkout`, which would resolve to `main` and false-PASS).
- [ ] The `optionality` job is added to `vote`'s `needs` so the run-level conclusion
      aggregation sees a terminal optionality result before Verified is cast.
- [ ] NO `secrets: inherit` on the reusable workflow; Gerrit values passed as plain
      inputs only (prevents fork-PR exposure of GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY via the PR trigger).
- [ ] The third-party `checkout-gerrit-change-action` is SHA-pinned to the same v1.0.2
      commit gerrit-verify uses.
- [ ] `_optionality.yml` declares `permissions: contents: read`; the optionality caller
      job is pure `uses:` (no `steps:`) and independent of clear-vote/require-ticket.
- [ ] Workflows parse/lint clean (actionlint if available) and the push-triggered
      optionality run passes on the change; gerrit-verify runs the optionality job and
      the Verified vote reflects it.

file_impact.reason: new reusable workflow_call workflow holding the optionality jobs
becomes thin push/PR caller of the reusable workflow
add optionality job + add to vote needs
new composite: branch-head vs gerrit-patchset checkout

compiled_state.title: Add optionality checks to the Gerrit Verified path via a workflow_call reusable workflow
